title: Sponsorship agreement holders: How to sponsor a refugee - Canada.ca
content:
## How to sponsor a refugee
You can sponsor 3 different kinds of refugees as a sponsorship agreement holder (SAH).

## Sponsor refugees you already know
You can sponsor refugees who are:
- members of a family in your community
- identified by an overseas contact
When you choose a refugee like this, we call them sponsor-referred refugees . Sponsor-referred refugees must meet the definition of a refugee .
How to sponsor a refugee you know

## Sponsor pre-approved refugees in need
The Blended Visa Office-Referred (BVOR) Program helps refugees with the greatest need to come to Canada. It also:
- splits the cost of financial support between government and sponsors
- connects sponsors with refugees we’ve already screened This means they can travel sooner.
- This means they can travel sooner.
How to sponsor a refugee through BVOR

## Sponsor a refugee with special needs
The Joint Assistance Sponsorship (JAS) Program helps refugees with special needs to come to Canada. The government usually supports them financially for 24 months. As a SAH you’ll provide:
- settlement assistance
How to sponsor a refugee through JAS

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
